segments.text: After this Job began speaking, cursing the day of his birth.
He said,
Wipe out the day I was born, and the night when it was announced that a boy had been conceived.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Turn that day to darkness. God above should not remember it. Don't let light shine on it.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Take it back, darkness and death-shadow. A black cloud should overshadow it. It should be as terrifying as the darkness of an eclipse during the day.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Blot out that night as if it never existed. Don't count it on the calendar. Don't let it have a day in any month.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Let that night be childless, with no sounds of happiness heard.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Those who place curses on certain days should curse it, those who have the power to raise Leviathan.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Its early morning stars should stay dark. Looking for light, may none come, may it not see the glimmer of dawn
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: for it did not shut my mother's womb to prevent me from seeing trouble.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Why wasn't I stillborn? Why didn't I die at birth?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Why was there a lap for me to lie on, or breasts for me to suck?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: For now I would be lying down in peace. I would be sleeping and at rest,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: along with the kings of this world and their officials whose palaces now lie in ruins,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: or with noblemen who collected gold and filled their houses with silver.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Why wasn't I a miscarriage, buried in secret, a baby who never saw the light?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: There in the grave the wicked give no more trouble, and those whose strength is gone have their rest.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: There prisoners take it easy—they don't hear the commands of their oppressors.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Both small and great are there, and slaves are freed from their masters.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Why does God give life to those who are suffering, living bitterly miserable lives,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: those who are waiting for death that does not come and who are looking for death more desperately than hunting for treasure?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: They're so incredibly happy when they reach the grave!
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Why is light given to someone who doesn't know where they're going, someone God has fenced in?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: My groans are the bread I eat; my raging tears are the water I drink.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: For all that I feared has happened to me; everything that I dreaded has come upon me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I have no peace, no quiet, no rest. All that comes is rage.
segments.speaker: Job
